support.summary: No CEO action required; deal is mid RFI with no blocked lever
support.detail: Barnes & Noble is a $250k eSourcing+CLM opportunity in Formal Evaluation (close 2027-03-31), driven by active manager-level champion Allison Baker through a competitive RFI released 11 May. No pricing block, roadmap ask, presales guarantee, or exec-connect need has surfaced. The open gap is rep-level EB mapping (identify who approved the FY procurement-tech budget); that is standard RSD/VP work, not CEO-irreplaceable.
support.metric: $250k ARR, EB status = gap (0 buyer-side contact roles)
support.why_not_vp: EB introductions, RFI debrief chasing and IT multi-threading are standard RSD/VP motions; nothing demands the CEO's personal authority or a CEO-to-CEO peer relationship.

monitor.reason: No signal dated within the last 14 days to anchor any trigger; the 27-day quiet is consistent with the buyer's internal RFI-scoring cycle before shortlist, close date is far out (Mar 2027), and the outstanding item (RFI debrief/shortlist) is buyer-dependent.
